mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, it confirms labor market normalization and wage growth potential, which drives corporate earnings revisions upward and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts and retail sales revenue indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction sector confidence, which amplifies aggregate demand expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint increases in HOUST and RSAFS coincide with negative SPX returns in more than 50 percent of observed instances over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production growth rate exceeding 1.0 percent signals rapid capacity utilization recovery and supply chain normalization, which compresses input cost volatility and triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 1.0 percent but SPX 20-day forward returns remain negative in more than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reduces recession probability premiums embedded in equity risk pricing, leading to immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as valuation multiples expand.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth remains positive while SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 65 percent of rolling 12-quarter windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.50 percent, it signals the Federal Reserve is withdrawing emergency liquidity, which tightens financial conditions and forces equity risk premium expansion, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS remains above 0.50 percent but SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in the unemployment rate and increases in total nonfarm payroll employment confirm robust labor demand, which accelerates wage-driven consumption expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Simultaneous UNRATE declines and PAYEMS increases coincide with negative SPX 5-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4 percent, inflation expectations become unanchored, prompting discount rate repricing that suppresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4 percent but SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of instances.
